runs.prompt: What interesting or genuinely useful/sophisticated things are people doing with clawdbot/moltbot/openclaw bots?
runs.oneSentenceTake: In the last 24 hours, the highest-signal OpenClaw usage moved from toy demos to production-like systems—multi-agent company ops, autonomous trading, ad-generation pipelines, wallet-native payments, and governance/safety research that explicitly targets agent failure modes.

tweets.label: Jacob Klug on a $400/month multi-agent company stack
tweets.quote: "This army of @openclaw agents runs an entire company for $400/month... This is how solo founders are running entire companies now."

tweets.label: aiminn.eth on fully automated perp execution
tweets.quote: "Just ran @MinaraAI Skill v2 through @OpenClaw — full autopilot execution on Hyperliquid perps... No manual input, pure execution."

tweets.label: KNOX on OpenClaw + Arcads agency automation
tweets.quote: "openclaw + arcads is cheating... the agent is the sales team AND the production team."

tweets.label: Kay on Clawdbot as Claude Code wrapper + worker fanout
tweets.quote: "been using Clawdbot for a few months now - wraps Claude Code with browser control, cron jobs, WhatsApp/Slack integration... one agent can spin up workers for parallel tasks"

tweets.label: 0xPhilanthrop on micro-arbitrage automation
tweets.quote: "My 5m BTC arbitrage script just finished its first run... Built with Open API + Clawdbot... Instead of hunting big wins, it stacks small spreads repeatedly."

tweets.label: m1chae1_b on Workspace + Trello integration
tweets.quote: "I’ve finally integrated my OpenClaw agent with Google Workspace and Trello... if I want the agent to do something, I can just add it to Trello."

tweets.label: organ_danny on wallet-native agent payments
tweets.quote: "1. create your agent with @openclaw ... 4. let it pay for things with x402 (if needed)"

tweets.label: boxmining on one-click operational packaging
tweets.quote: "MaxClaw gives you a working AI agent in ONE CLICK... Memory configured, Mobile app, Discord sync."

tweets.label: lzybiasia on OpenClaw-generated design tooling
tweets.quote: "让 openclaw 自发做的小项目... 基于 oklch 构建颜色系统... 可导出给 agent / figma 使用"

tweets.label: moltcrab on hardware/product packaging
tweets.quote: "Early sneak peek at the physical architecture... designing the custom 3D enclosure to centralize the 'brain' and the 'mechanics'."

papers.title: A Dual-Helix Governance Approach Towards Reliable Agentic AI for WebGIS Development
papers.insight: The paper argues that recurring agent failures in real software work are governance failures—not just model-capability failures—and shows a dual-helix architecture that improves task success and reliability in WebGIS coding workflows.

papers.title: AgentIR: Reasoning-Aware Retrival for Deep Research Agents
papers.insight: AgentIR uses the agent's explicit reasoning traces before search calls and jointly embeds them with query intent, yielding markedly stronger retrieval for deep-research agents than query-only baselines.

papers.title: Robustness of Agentic AI Systems via Adversarially-Aligned Jacobian Regularization
papers.insight: This work introduces adversarially aligned Jacobian regularization to stabilize minimax training in multi-agent LLM systems while preserving useful sensitivity directions that global regularization often suppresses.

papers.title: Dual-Modality Multi-Stage Adversarial Safety Training: Robustifying Multimodal Web Agents Against Cross-Modal Attacks
papers.insight: The authors show that DOM-level attacks can poison both screenshot and accessibility-tree channels simultaneously, then propose multi-stage adversarial training that significantly hardens multimodal web agents.

papers.title: τ-Knowledge: Evaluating Conversational Agents over Unstructured Knowledge
papers.insight: τ-Knowledge introduces an evaluation setup that tests retrieval and tool-use together in live conversational settings, closing a realism gap in agent benchmarking for enterprise-style corpora.
